findings.evidence: Roadmap B1 says `∫ e^{a|x|}|g| < ∞` for an L¹ function `g` and Part C says `g ∈ L²(measureT)` yields an L¹ function with all exponential moments; Mathlib defines `measureT` as a restriction to `Set.Ioc (-1) 1`, so the exponential factor is bounded on the support, while the PR grep hit at line 111 only states the unweighted measure moment.
findings.fix: Prove the general compact-support multiplier lemma first, e.g. for `{𝕜} [RCLike 𝕜] {g : ℝ → 𝕜}`, `Integrable g measureT → Integrable (fun x => (Real.exp (a * |x|) : 𝕜) • g x) measureT`, and derive the measure-only moment as the `g = 1` special case if still useful.
findings.issue: `integrable_exp_mul_abs_measureT` is the wrong level for the compact-support B1 consumer: it only proves `Integrable (fun x => exp (a * |x|)) measureT`, not the needed weighted-L¹ stability for a test function `g`.

summary: The added Chebyshev moment API is under-generalized for the roadmap target: it proves only a moment of the measure, while B1 needs exponential-weighted integrability after multiplying by an arbitrary L¹ test function supported by `measureT`. The L² normalized-mode lemmas are at the expected consumer level.
